English: You sure do!

Japanese: カワイイね！
English: No objections here!

Japanese: トミーさん。 ユカイなオジさんだよ！
English: That's Moe! He's such a funny clown!

Japanese: パパとね。ずいぶん ムカシからの友だちなの。
English: He's been a good friend to my dad for a really long time now.

Japanese: へえ‥‥団長さんと。
English: He was good friends with the Ringmaster?

Japanese: パパ、いつも言ってたよ。
English: My dad always said...

Japanese: 『サーカスのこと、まかせ られるのはアイツだけだ』って。
English: "When it comes to who I can entrust the circus to, it's definitely Moe."

Japanese: ふううん。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: そうだよね。すごく 楽しかったもん、あのピエロさん。
English: Yep! That clown is one lovable stooge.

Japanese: ね。なるほどくん！
English: Don't you think so, Nick?

Japanese: う‥‥うん。
English: Umm... No comment...

Japanese: あ、そのヒト。 ベンさん、だっけ？
English: Ahh, that guy. His name is Ben, right?

Japanese: あれ。知らないの？
English: Huh? You don't know him?

Japanese: ううん‥‥。あんまり 目立たないから。
English: I dunno... He didn't really catch my eye.

Japanese: リロくんのお友だち なんだよね？
English: He's friends with Trilo right?

Japanese: （ぼくたちに 聞かれてもなあ‥‥）
English: (Wait... We're the ones asking the questions around here...)

Japanese: “霊媒師”だっけ？ 真宵ちゃん。
English: Maya... You're a spirit channeler?

Japanese: うん！ まだ修行中なんだけどね。
English: Yep! I'm still training though.

Japanese: 楽しそうだよねえ。
English: That sounds like fun!

Japanese: そうだ！　ミリカたちと いっしょにやろうよ！
English: I think you should join us at the circus!

Japanese: え。‥‥霊媒を？
English: Huh? As a spirit channeler?

Japanese: きっとね。 サーカスの人気者になるよ！
English: Yeah! You'd be a big hit!

Japanese: ホント！ ねえ、ねえ。人気者だって！
English: Really!? She says I'd be a big hit!

Japanese: （なんてヘンジすれば いいんだよ‥‥？）
English: (I think she's just being nice.)

Japanese: あ。ムチ持ってるんだ このヒト。
English: Cool! This lady's got a whip!

Japanese: やっぱり、猛獣使いなの？
English: She's an animal tamer, huh?

Japanese: うーん‥‥どうなのかな。
English: Hmm... Sort of...

Japanese: （彼女に使われてるの、ぼくとか おとなしいジイさんだけど‥‥）
English: (Two women with whips... That's my cue to shut up and act like a good boy...)

Japanese: この人なんだけど‥‥。
English: What do you think about this person?

Japanese: ごめんね。
English: Sorry...
Sorry?

Japanese: ミリカ、もうココロに 決めたヒトがいるから。
English: I've already given my heart to another man.

Japanese: その人とは、おつきあい できないなあ。
English: So I'm not interested in enrolling in your dating service.

Japanese: そ‥‥それはザンネン。
English: Huh? I just wanted to know if you knew this person.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 タチミ・サーカス　テント内
English: December 28 Berry Big Circus Big Top

Japanese: どうなのかな、なるほどくん。
English: What do you think, Nick?

Japanese: あたしたち、ちょっとは 前に進んでるのかな。
English: I wonder if we've been making any progress...

Japanese: オソロシイこと言うなよ。 進んでるだろ？　そりゃ。
English: Don't be so negative... Of course we are making progress.

Japanese: でもさ。団長さん、みんなから 好かれていたみたいだし、
English: But... Everyone loved the Ringmaster...

Japanese: 現場の写真には ハンニンの足跡がないし‥‥
English: And there's no sign of footprints on the scene...

Japanese: ‥‥ナゾだらけだよ。
English: There's still a lot of mysteries left to be solved.

Japanese: たしかになあ。
English: Of course.

Japanese: それに、ミリカちゃんも いなくなっちゃったし。
English: And now Regina isn't here!

Japanese: それはカンケーないだろ。
English: I'm not seeing how that's related...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 タチミ・サーカス　テント内
English: December 28 Berry Big Circus Big Top

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: いったい、どうなってるの？ ベンさんとリロくん。
English: What in the world happened with Ben and Trilo?

Japanese: まったくだよ。あの人形、 何を見たっていう
English: Quite a pair, those two. What did that puppet see anyways?

Japanese: キーッ！
English: Oooooh!

Japanese: わわわ。 こ、今度はなんだ！
English: Oh no... Now what!?

Japanese: キッキーッ！
English: Oooo-Ooooh!

Japanese: ギャアアアアアアアアアアアアッ
English: Ahhhhh... Yeowwwwwwwww... Ahhhhhhhhhh...

Japanese: あっ！　気がついた？ なるほどくん！
English: What is it, Nick?

Japanese: あ、う‥‥うん。 い、いったい、あのサル‥‥
English: T-T-That monkey...

Japanese: あああっ！ ぼくの弁護士バッジが！
English: AHHHH! My badge!!

Japanese: あのサルに取られちまった！
English: That monkey stole it!!

Japanese: うえええええええっ！
English: WHHHAAATTT!?

Japanese: あははははは！
English: Hehehehehe!

Japanese: 見ーちゃった。 ベンゴシさんの、あわてたカオ。
English: Mr. Attorney, that face was so cute. You looked so completely dumbfounded!

Japanese: ミリカちゃん！
English: Regina!!

Japanese: き、きみか！　あのサルを‥‥！
English: You!! That monkey!!

Japanese: うーん。怒っちゃだめだよ。 ‥‥めっ！
English: Hey... No need to get angry... OK?

Japanese: で、で、でも！ ぼくの弁護士バッジが‥‥！
English: B-B-But!! My attorney's badge!!

Japanese: ふふ。ミリカが助けてあげるから。 ‥‥ね？
English: Don't worry... I'll help you out.

Japanese: う‥‥うん。 （どうも、この子にはヨワい）
English: OK... If you say so... (If I don't get my badge back, how can I flash it?)

Japanese: ルーサー、っていうんだよ。 あのサルくん。
English: By the way... The monkey's name is Money. Money the monkey.

Japanese: る、るーさー‥‥。
English: His name is Monke... Money?

Japanese: ルーサー‥‥っていうの？ あのサル。
English: The monkey... Is called Money?

Japanese: うん。
English: Yes.

Japanese: 弁護士バッジを取られたんだ。 返してくれないか。
English: Well, the rich ape just stole my attorney's badge. Mind if I get that back?

Japanese: ルーサーにはねえ、 ミリカも困ってるんだよ。
English: I'll see what I can do... Even I have Money problems.

Japanese: え。
English: Eh?

Japanese: ルーサー、ピカピカ光るモノを 持ってっちゃうの。おうちに。
English: Whenever Money sees something shiny, he takes it back home.

Japanese: へええ。おもしろいねえ。
English: Haha... That's pretty funny.

Japanese: じゃあ、そのサルのうちに つれてってよ。
English: Well, I guess I'll just have to find out where Money disappeared to...

Japanese: そうだねー。 トミーおじさんに聞くといいよ。
English: I think that's your best bet. You should probably ask Uncle Moe... He might know.

Japanese: え。‥‥どうして？
English: Huh? You don't know?

Japanese: あのねー。ルーサーは、 ミリカのお友だちじゃないから。
English: Well... Money isn't exactly someone I am on friendly terms with.

Japanese: えっ！
English: What!?

Japanese: だって、“猛獣”じゃないモン。 サルくんは。
English: He's not really the kind of animal I work with... Even if he does need taming.

Japanese: あー。そりゃそうか。
English: Oh, I see!

Japanese: （トミーおじさん‥‥か）
English: (Go to Moe's... Hmm...)

Japanese: （あのピエロに、また 会わなくちゃならないのかよ）
English: (I guess it is time that I revisit that kooky clown...)

Japanese: あの‥‥ ベンさんのことなんだけど。
English: Do you mind telling us a bit about Ben?

Japanese: ベンさん‥‥？
English: Ben...?

Japanese: ああ。リロくんと、 いつもいっしょにいるヒト？
English: You mean the guy that is always hanging around with Trilo?

Japanese: いっしょに、って‥‥。
English: What do you mean "hanging around"?

Japanese: ミリカ、おつきあいを申しこまれ ちゃったの。‥‥リロくんに。
English: Well, he was there when Trilo told me that he was in love with me...

Japanese: “リロくん”に？
English: Trilo told you he was in love with you?

Japanese: うん。リロくんて、 おもしろいでしょ？
English: Yes he did. Kinda cute, don't ya think?

Japanese: いなせだし、歌もじょうずだし。 ミリカ、大好き。
English: He's so smart, and he's such a wonderful singer... I love him!

Japanese: あの。ベンさんは‥‥？
English: But... What about Ben?

Japanese: ベンさんはカンケイないよ。 ミリカが好きなのは、リロくん。
English: What about Ben? He's got nothing to do with me loving Trilo.

Japanese: （アタマがいたくなってきた‥‥）
English: (Like sand through the hourglass, so are the Days of the Circus...)

Japanese: ね。ね。ミリカちゃん、 プロポーズされちゃったんでしょ？
English: Regina, you were proposed to, weren't you?

Japanese: プロポーズ？ ‥‥ううん。それはまだだよ。
English: Proposed to? Nope... That won't be for a while.

Japanese: あれ、そうなの？ ‥‥ヘンだね、なるほどくん。
English: Huh? Really? That's strange, isn't it Nick?

Japanese: マックスもリロくんも 宣言してたからなあ。
English: Yeah... Max and Trilo both said otherwise.

Japanese: “ケッコンするんだあ”って。
English: They said they asked for her hand in marriage.

Japanese: あ。でも、マックスは 団長さんに話しただけだから。
English: Ahh, but Max only talked to the Ringmaster about it.

Japanese: ああ。おヤクソクの “娘さんをください”ってヤツ。
English: I forgot about that. He asked the Ringmaster for her hand, not Regina directly.

Japanese: リロくんも、まだプロポーズは してないみたいだね。
English: So I guess Trilo hasn't asked her directly yet.

Japanese: わああ。 プロポーズしてくれるんだあ。
English: What!? He's going to propose to me!?

Japanese: ミリカ、迷っちゃうよ。 ‥‥ねえ、真宵ちゃん！
English: I'm so confused... How about you Maya?

Japanese: え？　なあに？
English: Huh? What?

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん、どっちがいいと思う？ マックスと、リロくん。
English: Who do you think I should go for? Max or Trilo?

Japanese: ‥‥で、でも‥‥。 人形だよ？　リロくんは。
English: Wait... Wait... You do realize that Trilo is... a... puppet?
Uh?

Japanese: ミリカは、そんなコト 気にしないけど。
English: I don't care that he's a bit stiff...

Japanese: はあああ‥‥。
English: Oh boy.

Japanese: これなんだけど‥‥
English: Take a look at this...

Japanese: だ、ダメだよなるほどくん！ そんな写真見せちゃあ。
English: NICK! YOU CAN'T SHOW HER THAT PICTURE!!

Japanese: あ‥‥、そうだね。
English: Ouch... Yeah, that would be a bit insensitive of me.

Japanese: なあに？ ミリカにも見せてよー。
English: Huh? Show me! Show me! Show me!

Japanese: マックスってさ、 どうやって飛んでるのかなあ。
English: Do you know how Max flies in the sky like he does?

Japanese: ミリカ、知ってるよ。 教えてあげようか？
English: I do know! Want me to tell ya?

Japanese: ホント！
English: You know!?

Japanese: ふふ‥‥。 じゃあ、真宵ちゃんだけだよ。
English: Uh-huh. *giggle* But you have to keep it secret!

Japanese: え。
English: Eh?

Japanese: んーとね。‥‥ごにょごにょ。
English: So here's how it works... *mumble* *mumble*

Japanese: えーっ。そうだったんだあ！ すっごいなあ！
English: Wow! That's incredible!!

Japanese: なな、泣かないで！ なるほどくん。
English: Oh, don't pout Nick!

Japanese: ふふ。冗談だよお。ミリカだって 知らないよ、魔術なんて。
English: Hehe... We were just teasing you! I don't know a thing about magic!!

Japanese: ごめんね、なるほどくん。
English: Sorry we tricked you Nick.

Japanese: あー、リロくん。
English: Awww... Trilo.

Japanese: ‥‥動かないね。 おヒルネかな。
English: He's not moving... Maybe he's taking a nap.

Japanese: ベンさんに 返してあげないとね。
English: We have to take him back to Ben.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 テント内　団長室
English: December 28 Big Top Ringmaster's Room

Japanese: ここが、団長さんの‥‥？
English: This was the Ringmaster's room?

Japanese: そう。 ‥‥被害者の部屋、だね。
English: Yes. This room belonged to the victim.

Japanese: きのうの夜、マックスはこの部屋で 団長に会っているはずだ。
English: Which means this must be where Max met the Ringmaster last night.

Japanese: そういえば そんなこと言ってたね。
English: Now that you mention it, that is what he said.

Japanese: それにしても‥‥ すごいな、ポスター。
English: I wonder what... Hmm... That's an intresting poster.

Japanese: あっ！ マックスのポスターがあるよ！
English: Ah! It's a poster of Max!

Japanese: ほしい。なるほどくん。
English: I want it! I want it! Nick! I want it!

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥。
English: I wanna get outta here...

Japanese: 来客用のテーブル‥‥ 書類がおいてあるな。
English: It's a table for guests... There are some papers scattered on top.

Japanese: あ、これ。‥‥マックスの 出演料が書いてあるよ。
English: Ah! Look at this! Max's salary is written on this piece of paper.

Japanese: ぐッ！
English: YIKES!

Japanese: ど、どうした？
English: W-What is it?

Japanese: こ、こんなにもうかるんだ ‥‥魔術師って‥‥
English: I didn't know that a magician... This salary is incredible!

Japanese: （オドロキのあまり、 ちょっとヨロめいている）
English: (She looks like she's about ready to pass out from shock...)

Japanese: どれどれ？ ‥‥うッ！
English: How much is it!? How much is it!? T-T-THAT MUCH!?

Japanese: ね？
English: Incredible, huh?

Japanese: た、たしかに。
English: You can say that again.

Japanese: （これは、ギャラ値上げ 交渉の書類だ）
English: (This must be the paper they used to negotiate Max's salary...)

Japanese: （団長のサインがしてある。 日付は‥‥？）
English: (The Ringmaster signed and dated it...)

Japanese: どうしたの？　なるほどくん。
English: What's the matter, Nick?

Japanese: マックスのギャラが 上がってるんだよ。
English: Max definitely got a raise...

Japanese: ‥‥事件の１週間前にね‥‥。
English: But this document is dated a week ago!

Japanese: 証拠品《団長の書類》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Ringmaster's Papers added to the Court Record.

Japanese: いっぱいあるよねー、 ポスター。
English: There's a lot of posters here don't you think?

Japanese: そうだね。
English: There are indeed.

Japanese: これなら、１枚ぐらいなくなっても わかんないねー。
English: So many posters that they aren't likely to miss one, are they?

Japanese: ダメだぞ。 勝手にもらっちゃ。
English: Maya... We're supposed to be the honest ones around here.

Japanese: だってホラ、なるほどくんだって 気づいてないでしょ？
English: But... But... You didn't even notice that I took one!

Japanese: （ムム‥‥。すでに１枚、 シッケイしていたらしい）
English: (Arrghhh... She already swiped one!)

Japanese: えへへ。
English: Heh! Heh!

Japanese: えへへ、じゃないよ‥‥。
English: You're incorrigible, you know that?

Japanese: 《マックスのポスター》を 真宵ちゃんがくすねた。
English: Max G. Promo Poster added to the Court Record.

Japanese: あ。これって“エンビ服” っていうんだよ。
English: You may not know this, but they call this a "tailcoat".

Japanese: 知ってるよ。 ‥‥？
English: And they call this "the face of someone who already knew that..." Hmm...

Japanese: どうしたの？
English: What?

Japanese: ポケットから、白い 紙切れがのぞいてるね。
English: A scrap of white paper is sticking out of the coat pocket.

Japanese: ほほお。どれどれ。
English: Huh? Where? Where?

Japanese: ダメだよ、真宵ちゃん。
English: Calm down, Maya.

Japanese: 勝手にヒトのポケット 探っちゃあ。
English: You can't just go rummaging through people's coats!

Japanese: ううう。あたしばっかり ワルモノにする‥‥
English: Ahhh... You always make me feel like I'm doing something wrong...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 テント内　団長室
English: December 28 Big Top Ringmaster's Room

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらず、 ゴチャゴチャしてるねー。
English: It looks the same as always... A great big mess.

Japanese: これだったら、もう１枚ぐらい ポスターがなくなっても‥‥。
English: Considering how messy it is, I bet they wouldn't notice if another poster went missing.

Japanese: いやいやいや。やめておきなよ。
English: Will you just stop it you poster pilferer!

Japanese: 冗談だよ。マックスのポスターは もう、もらっちゃったし。
English: I'm just kidding! You know I already got one of these posters!!

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥。
English: You mean stole one of those posters!

Japanese: そういえばマックスさん、 言ってたよね。
English: Yeah... Uh... Let's focus on what Max told us.

Japanese: この部屋にリロをかくした、って。
English: He said that he hid Trilo somewhere in this room.

Japanese: （リロ‥‥ ああ、腹話術の人形ね‥‥）
English: (Trilo... Ohh! The ventriloquist's puppet!)

Japanese: お‥‥。タナの下に 何か押しこまれてるぞ！
English: Hmm... There's something shoved under the bookshelf!

Japanese: ‥‥これは‥‥
English: This... is...

Japanese: それ、リロじゃない？ ベンさんの人形！
English: That's Trilo! That's Ben's puppet!

Japanese: そうみたいだ。 あとで、返しておこうか。
English: I think you're right. We'll give it back to him later.

Japanese: 《リロ》を真宵ちゃんに持たせた。
English: Trilo Quist handed over to Maya.

Japanese: なんであたしが持つの！
English: Why do I have to carry this thing!?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 テント内　食堂
English: December 28 Big Top Cafeteria

Japanese: うわ。きたないなあ。
English: Eww... This place is gross.

Japanese: きのうの夕食のあとだね、こりゃ。
English: This must be because of last night.

Japanese: ゆうべの事件で、かたづける ヒマもなかったんじゃないかな。
English: They didn't have time to clean up after dinner because of the murder.

Japanese: そういえば、トミーさん、 なんか言ってたよね。
English: That reminds me... What was it that Moe said?

Japanese: きのうの朝、ここでマックスが ベンさんのアタマを殴った、とか。
English: He said that yesterday morning Max "clonked" Ben over the head here.

Japanese: それに、“おもしろいモノが アル・カポネ”って。
English: He also said that, "There's Gotti be something interesting there."

Japanese: ね。なるほどくん。 ナニ？　“ある・かぽね”って。
English: Nick? What's "Gotti be interesting?"

Japanese: ‥‥知らなくても、 なんのモンダイもないよ。
English: Don't ask...

Japanese: ふうん。
English: Hmph!

Japanese: （‥‥ん？　これは、 ジュースのビン、かな？）
English: (What's this? ...Must be a juice bottle or something?)

Japanese: あーあ。あぶないなあ。
English: Ahhh... Watch out Nick! Broken glass on the floor is dangerous!

Japanese: でも、ふつうに落としたぐらいじゃ 割れないぞ、ビンなんて。
English: Hmm... A broken bottle just lying in the middle of the floor.

Japanese: じゃあ、なんで割れてるの？
English: Do you think it means anything?

Japanese: ‥‥“おもしろいモノが アル・カポネ”‥‥か。
English: "There's Gotti be something interesting there."
Huh?

Japanese: どうやらもう一度、 会う必要がありそうだな‥‥彼と。
English: Looks like we're going to have to go back and meet with him.

Japanese: かれ‥‥？
English: Him?

Japanese: 証拠品《割れたビン》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Broken Bottle added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 テント内　食堂
English: December 28 Big Top Cafeteria

Japanese: あっ。ベンさん！
English: Oh! Hey Ben!

Japanese: お！　‥‥えと、うグ ‥‥こんにちは‥‥
English: A... Umm... Uhh... H-H-Hello.

Japanese: こんにちはー。 ここもサムいですねー。
English: Hello to you too! It's awfully cold today don't you agree?

Japanese: そ。そ。‥‥うで‥‥すね‥‥
English: Y-Ye-Ye... Uhh... I-I d-do indeed.

Japanese: サムいって。なるほどくん。
English: Don't you think it's cold Nick?

Japanese: （この人の話を聞いてると、 妙にチカラが入るな‥‥）
English: (I don't see how talking about the weather is helping our case.)

Japanese: あの。えと、どうもスミマセン‥‥ リロくんがその‥‥
English: Umm... Uhh... Thanks... Now that Trilo's here...

Japanese: “リロくんが”って‥‥ベンさんが しゃべらせてるんじゃあ？
English: "Now that Trilo's here..." Does that mean you can talk normally now?

Japanese: オイ！　このヤロー。
English: Hey! Buttface!

Japanese: わっ。
English: AHHH!

Japanese: 明日が楽しみだなあ、 ベンゴシくん！
English: You must be looking forward to tomorrow, aren't you Mr. Ambulance Chaser!?
Uhh...

Japanese: オレな。あのイケスカねえ 手品師を消してやるんだ！
English: You know... It's time to get rid of that pesky magician once and for all!!

Japanese: リ‥‥リロくん‥‥？
English: Tri... Trilo?

Japanese: じゃ、そういうコトだ。 法廷で会おうぜ！
English: Enough jibba jabba! Let's get to court already!

Japanese: あ、ま、待って‥‥！
English: Ahh... Hey! Wait a second!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。 どういうコトかな‥‥？
English: Nick... What's going on?

Japanese: 証人、てことだろうね。 ‥‥明日の裁判の。
English: He's a witness for tomorrow's trial...

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Ahhhhh...

Japanese: 事件のことなんですけど。
English: Tell us what you know about the murder...

Japanese: い。いえ。 ボク‥‥そんな。あの‥‥
English: I-I-I-I... Uhhh... I-I-I-I uhhh...

Japanese: 何か、ご存じないですか？
English: Do you know anything at all?

Japanese: ごぞ、ごぞ、ごぞ、 ‥‥んじないで‥‥す‥‥
English: I d-d-d-don't k-know a-a-a-a t-t-thing!

Japanese: あのー‥‥
English: Well...

Japanese: えと、し知りませェェんッ‥‥
English: W-W-Well... I-I-I-I don't k-k-know...

Japanese: もう、長いんですか？ このサーカスに来てから。
English: You've been in the circus for a long time, haven't you Ben?

Japanese: そ、う‥‥ですね。 よ。よ、４年ぐらい‥‥
English: Y-Y-Yes... Ab-Ab-About f-f-four years...

Japanese: あたしも、 気に入っちゃいました‥‥ここ。
English: I gotta admit, I'm kinda interested in this place as well.

Japanese: トミーさんはおもしろいし、 マックスはスゴいし、
English: Moe is so funny, and Max is so amazing...

Japanese: ミリカちゃんはかわいいし‥‥。
English: Not to mention Regina being the cutest little thing I have ever seen!

Japanese: ‥‥ミリカ‥‥さん、ですか‥‥ ボ‥‥クはちょ‥‥っと‥‥
English: ...Regina? I... Umm... I...

Japanese: ニガ、ニガ‥‥ニガテです‥‥
English: D-D-D-Don't R-R-Really... L-L-L-Like... H-Her... T-T-Too... M-Much...

Japanese: あ、あれ。 ‥‥そうなんですか？
English: Hmm... Are you sure about that?

Japanese: （‥‥おかしいな）
English: (Hmm... That's odd...)

Japanese: （アイを語ったとかなんとか、 マックスが言ってたような‥‥）
English: (Max said something about a confession of love...)

Japanese: 事件のことなんですけど‥‥
English: Tell us what you know about the murder...

Japanese: どこ見て話してんだよ！ コッチだろコッチ！
English: You talkin' to me? I said you talkin' to me? Don't look at him when you're talkin' to me!

Japanese: ダメだよリロくん‥‥
English: Trilo Quist. You behave young man!

Japanese: うるせえ！
English: Shut up Woody!

Japanese: 事件って、アレだろ？ ここの団長がやられた。
English: What murder are you talking about? You mean the one where they off'ed the old man?

Japanese: そうだけど。
English: I guess so...

Japanese: ケッ！　さわぐほどのコトかよ！
English: No need to make such a fuss about things!

Japanese: ケチなギャラでコキつかいやがって ‥‥あのタヌキ！
English: That old mutt paid us all peanuts!

Japanese: だ、ダメだよリロくん。 そんなコト‥‥
English: Trilo... You can't say things like that! I didn't raise you to be that kind of puppet!

Japanese: うるせえ！
English: Don't you have nerve pills or something to take right now?

Japanese: ‥‥すさんだコンビだね、 なるほどくん。
English: These two are really an odd couple, aren't they Nick?

Japanese: 聞いたぜ。あの、キザなヤローが パクられたってな‥‥ヒヒ！
English: OK! OK! I'll talk... Gramps got clobbered over the head!!

Japanese: ダンチョーを殴ったのは、 あのキザヤローだぜ！
English: The Ringmaster got knocked upside his cheap head by that flying fraud!!

Japanese: マックス・ギャラクティカ‥‥？ ど、どうしてだい？
English: You mean Max Galactica? Why do you say that?

Japanese: ダメだよ！　リロくん。 い、イイカゲンなコトを‥‥
English: Trilo! Straighten up!! Don't accuse people like that!

Japanese: ナニ言ってんだよベン！ オメエもいただろ、あそこに！
English: Straighten up!? I'm made of wood!! Besides, you were there! You know what happened!

Japanese: あそこ‥‥？
English: You were there...?

Japanese: ヒヒッ！　気が向いたら 教えてやるよ！
English: Hehe! If you're that interested, then I'll let you in on the facts!

Japanese: ッたく、シケたトコだぜ。
English: Let me lay it all out for you...

Japanese: ギャラは安い、ピエロはサムい、 あいぼうはポンコツ。
English: The pay sucks... The clown sucks... And my partner has his hand up my pants.

Japanese: あいぼうって‥‥ベンさん？
English: Your partner... You mean Ben?

Japanese: おじょうちゃん。このクズにさ、 言ってやってくれよ。
English: Yeah... The creepy old guy who never finds it in himself to leave me alone...

Japanese: もっとシャンとしろ！　ってよぉ。 こんなサイテー男、タクサンだぜ！
English: Tell him to "BACK OFF" for me will ya? He's just another one of the dorks around here!
Oh... My...

Japanese: ‥‥でもよ。 こんなゴミためにもよぉ‥‥、
English: But I'll be fair... In this cesspool of human garbage masquerading as

Japanese: いたんだよなぁ。 ‥‥オレのマドンナが。
English: circus performers... I found my Madonna.

Japanese: “オレの”‥‥
English: Your...

Japanese: “まどんな”‥‥？
English: Madonna?

Japanese: ミリカだよ、ミリカ。 あの女はイケてるぜ。なあ、ベン。
English: Regina. My lovely Regina. She is stunning... Right? Ben?

Japanese: い、いや‥‥ボクはチョット、 ああいうのは‥‥。
English: Well... I'm not sure if I would go that far...

Japanese: かー。コレだからなコイツ。 一生やってろ！
English: You'll have to excuse him... He does not understand of what he speaks.

Japanese: オレはケッコンするぜ！ あのマドンナとな！
English: I, on the other hand, am an appreciator of true beauty. Hence why I shall marry her!

Japanese: けけ‥‥ケッコン！
English: Ma-Ma-Ma-Marry!?

Japanese: あの‥‥ケッコン‥‥するの？ ミリカちゃんと。
English: You... You're going to marry... Regina?

Japanese: ああ、そうだぜ。向こうも マンザラじゃねえみたいだしな。
English: That's right! She doesn't quite realize the joy that awaits her, does she?

Japanese: （たしかに、“悩んでる”とか 言ってたな。オソロシイことに）
English: (But she did seem a bit worried about things...)

Japanese: い、いや、そ、それは‥‥
English: Well... She...

Japanese: 悪ィかよケッコンしちゃ！ オレの勝手だろ！
English: I don't care! It is my choice not hers! We're getting hitched!

Japanese: そりゃ‥‥そうだケド‥‥
English: I know you think that... But...

Japanese: （そ、そうなのか‥‥？）
English: (...But what...?)

Japanese: オレな。歌をプレゼントしたんだ！ マドンナに。
English: I gave her a special gift... I gave her the wonderful gift of song...

Japanese: うた？
English: You gave her a song?

Japanese: テノール歌手だからオレ。
English: Well, I am a renowned tenor!

Japanese: いいか。おじょうちゃんにも 聞かせてやるよ。
English: You'll be happy to know that I've decided to grace you with one of my songs!

Japanese: ‥‥エヘン！
English: Mi mi mi mi mi mi mi!

Japanese: お～うるわしの～キミの目玉に～ チュッと～くちづけ～したい～‥‥
English: "I want you to touch me! I want you to kiss me! I want you to..."

Japanese: ‥‥ってかァ！
English: The rest is private!

Japanese: へええ。メロディがいいなあ。
English: Well... Umm... The melody is pretty good.

Japanese: 歌詞はサイアクだけどね。
English: But those lyrics... I think they need a little work.

Japanese: いいか！　オレはやるぜ！
English: Who asked you!? I'm the artist here!

Japanese: あ、そうだ。 これ、ベンさんのですよね？
English: Ahh... Ben... This is yours isn't it?

Japanese: うおッ！　あ。お。す‥‥ スミマセン。ボ、ボクのです。
English: Y-Y-Yes!! T-That's mine!

Japanese: はい、どうぞ。
English: Here ya go!

Japanese: 《リロ》をベンさんに返した。
English: Trilo Quist returned to Ben.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、真宵ちゃん。 行こうか。
English: Alright Maya... Let's get going!

Japanese: そうだね。 じゃあ、さよならベンさん。
English: It's that time isn't it? See ya around, Ben!

Japanese: は‥‥ハイ。
English: O-O-O-OK...

Japanese: ‥‥さ。なるほどくん。 次はどこへ行こうか？
English: So Nick... Where are we going next?

Japanese: そうだね。 もう一度、あのピエロの‥‥
English: Let's see... Maybe we should go talk to the clown once again.

Japanese: おいッ！　待てよ！
English: HEY! WAIT!!

Japanese: だ、だれ‥‥今の。
English: Who said that?

Japanese: どこ見てんだよ！ こっちだギザギザヤロー！
English: WHAT ARE YOU LOOKIN' AT? I'M RIGHT HERE YOU BLIND WENCH!

Japanese: なんだよテメェら。 アイサツもなしか、このヤロー。
English: What's your problem anyways? Don't you know how to properly greet someone!?

Japanese: べべ‥‥ベベベンベンさん‥‥？
English: Ben? Is that you Ben?

Japanese: い、いえいえいえ！　いえいえッ！ ボ、ボクはそんな‥‥。
English: NO! NO! NO! NO! NO! NO! NO! I would never...

Japanese: “ベンさん”じゃねえんだよ！ どこ見てんだ、こっちこっち！
English: It was me! Yeah me! Down here!!

Japanese: き‥‥きみは、リロ‥‥。
English: You... You're... Trilo!?

Japanese: 呼び捨てにすんじゃねぇよ！ リロ“様”だろ！
English: That is Mr. Quist to you sir! Learn some manners before you just blurt out my name!

Japanese: ホレ、やりなおし！
English: Now try speaking to me again, but this time with some proper respect!

Japanese: （‥‥まま、またかい‥‥） リロ様‥‥なのか？
English: (Not again... *sigh*) Mr. Quist... Is that better?

Japanese: ったく、どこに目ェつけてんだ このヤロー。
English: No! Look at me when I am talking to you, you 8-bit excuse for an attorney!

Japanese: だ、ダメだよリロくん、 そんなコト。
English: Trilo! We talked about insulting people! You promised!!

Japanese: テメェもテメェだ！ そんなだから、ナメられるんだよ！
English: But he was mocking me! Being mean to bullies was not included in the deal!

Japanese: ゴメンよリロくん‥‥。
English: I'm sorry Trilo...

Japanese: どど‥‥どうなってるの なるほどくん！
English: Nick! What just happened?

Japanese: リロ、って人形でしょ？ ‥‥腹話術の
English: Trilo is still a puppet right? A ventriloquist's puppet!

Japanese: コラ、そこ！　呼びすてにすんな って言ってんじゃねえかよ！
English: Hey! Who do you think you are, calling me a puppet!?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 宿舎１階　トミーの部屋
English: December 28 Lodging Hall 1st Floor Moe's Room

Japanese: ここは‥‥？
English: This is it?

Japanese: ドアのプレートには “トミー”って書いてあったけど。
English: The nametag on the door says "Moe" on it.

Japanese: るす‥‥みたいだね。
English: I guess he's not here...

Japanese: それにしても、 散らかってるよねー。
English: Wow... It's a real mess in here!

Japanese: （ぼくの部屋より マシだと思うけどなあ）
English: (My room's probably worse though...)

Japanese: ‥‥ま、いいや。 また、あとで来てみようか。
English: Oh well... I give up. We'll have to come back later.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 宿舎１階　トミーの部屋
English: December 28 Lodging Hall 1st Floor Moe's Room

Japanese: こんにちは‥‥。
English: Hello...?

Japanese: パンパカパーン！
English: KABLAMMO!

Japanese: おめでとーございます！ ‥‥あなたこそがッ！
English: CONGRATULATIONS! YOU'RE THE BIG WEINER!

Japanese: トミーの部屋におとずれた、 １００万人目のお客サマでーす！
English: The one millionth visitor to the room of one Mr. Moe Curls, AKA ME!!

Japanese: （うう‥‥デカい声‥‥）
English: (Earplugs... Must... Find... Earplugs...)

Japanese: 記念に、このブドウ、 １つぶどう？　‥‥なーんて。
English: To celebrate this momentous occasion, would you care for an organic grape? Just one!

Japanese: ‥‥今の、シャレ。わかる？
English: Did you get my joke right there!?

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃ。あひゃ。 あひゃひゃあひゃあひゃひゃ‥‥
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: しかもこのブドウ、 巨峰よ巨峰。‥‥キョホーッ！
English: I welched on giving you more than one!!

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: ダメダメ。ダメだよぉ、 おかしかったら笑わないと。
English: No no no! If it was funny, it is your duty as a human being to laugh!!

Japanese: 笑えないヤツは、中国へ 行っチャイナ！　‥‥なーんて。
English: People who don't laugh are usually last-seen in Lan-sing. Catch my drift?

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃ。 あひゃひゃひゃひゃひゃ！
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃん？
English: Umm... Maya?

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃあひゃ。 あひゃひゃあひゃ。あひゃひゃ！
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: （あ、アクムだ‥‥）
English: (This is like some Faustian nightmare...)

Japanese: いやー、笑った笑った。
English: C'mon! It was funny!

Japanese: やっぱりピエロさんは おかしいねー、なるほどくん。
English: Clowns are always funny in my book!

Japanese: たしかにおかしいね。 ‥‥どこか。
English: In my book, they're just funny lookin'.

Japanese: おぢょーさん。いいねその、 和服のコーディネート！
English: You sure do have great taste in clothes girlie! Look at that garb!

Japanese: やっぱりコーディネートは、 こーでねーと！　‥‥なーんて。
English: You look just like Gretta Garb... OH!

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃひゃあひゃ あひゃあひゃひゃあひゃ！
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: 帰ろう。
English: *sigh* I'm goin' home...

Japanese: だ、ダメだよなるほどくん！
English: No! Nick! You can't!!

Japanese: ぼくは、つまらない ギャグはユルせる。
English: You know, I can excuse a bad joke or two...

Japanese: でも、それを自分で笑うヤツは ガマンならないんだ！
English: But this stooge keeps laughing at his own jokes! That's what I object to!

Japanese: ま。ま。いいじゃない。 なんてったって、おもしろいし。
English: OK OK... I get it. But you have to admit he is kinda funny...
(Argghhh... No, I do not have to admit that, because he isn't!)

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃひゃ。 あひゃ。ひゃあひゃひゃあひゃ！
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: あ、そうだ。
English: Ahh yes...

Japanese: サーカスのテント、 ちょっと広いからな。
English: The Berry Big Circus is very big isn't it?

Japanese: こいつ、持って行きな。 サーカスの見取り図だ。
English: You should always carry a map with you to get around...

Japanese: わあ。ありがとうございます！ って、これ世界地図ですよ！
English: Ah! Thank you!! Umm... This is an atlas!!

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃひゃ。 あっひゃひゃあひゃひゃ。
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: いやー、まちがった。 ‥‥めんごめんご。
English: Oh... I kill myself. Really. I'm dying here... CORONARY! CORONARY!

Japanese: （ワザとにせよ、そうでないに せよ、笑えない‥‥）
English: (Now he's just laughing to hear his own voice...)

Japanese: 証拠品《サーカスの見取り図》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Circus Map added to the Court Record.

Japanese: このサーカスのコト、 聞かせてください。
English: Could you please tell us more about the Berry Big Circus?

Japanese: ほいきたガッテン ショーチのスケ！
English: It's a Berry Big story... You sure you got that kind of time?

Japanese: （‥‥ギャグはホント、 よけいなんだけどな‥‥）
English: (And the hits just keep on comin'... *sigh*)

Japanese: このサーカスはね。もう２０年も やってるんだよ。
English: This circus has been in business for 20 years.

Japanese: 立見　七百人（たちみなおと）団長 のもとに芸人が集まってさあ。
English: We all performed under the guidance of the Ringmaster, Russell Berry.
Twenty years!? Wow!

Japanese: 正直、サーカスは ラクじゃなかったよ。
English: Working in the circus is never easy, especially nowadays...

Japanese: 映画やテレビ、ボウリング。 楽しいコトはいくらでもあるから。
English: With movies, TV, and bowling, there is just too much competition.

Japanese: でもあたし、 サーカス好きですよ！
English: But... But... I LOVE THE CIRCUS!

Japanese: オレっちもさ。だから２０年、 つづけられたんだ。
English: I love it too... That is why I've been here for 20 years.

Japanese: カンバンを守りとおして、 今夜もカンバンわ！　なーんて。
English: We work hard to keep the show running. No one sends in the clowns on us!

Japanese: ダメだよ、なるほどくん。 ホラ、笑って！
English: Nick! He just made a joke! Laugh!!

Japanese: はははははははははははははは。
English: Har! Har! Har! Har! Har! Har!

Japanese: 団長は、エラいヒトだったよ。
English: The Ringmaster was a real big shot in the circus world. A real class act.

Japanese: 客が入らないときは、自バラを 切って給料をくれたもんだ。
English: Even when there were no customers, Russell would use his own money to pay me.

Japanese: オレっちも、そのころは 家族があったからな。
English: Because he knew that I had a family to care for...

Japanese: ありがたく、ちょうだいしたよ。
English: He was happy to take care of his employees.

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: いったい‥‥ ダレが、あんないい人を‥‥！
English: How could anyone do that to such a wonderful man...!?

Japanese: トミーさん。
English: Moe...

Japanese: なんじゃらホイ。
English: Wiggidty-wiggidty-wiggidty WHAT?
Ugh...

Japanese: ごめんなさいトミーさん。
English: I'm sorry Moe.

Japanese: なるほどくんて、 ユーモアのセンスがなくて。
English: Nick was born without a sense of humor.

Japanese: はは。いいよ気にしなくて。
English: Don't worry about it... How can you fault someone for being born that way?

Japanese: 事件のことなんですけど‥‥。
English: Let's talk about the murder.

Japanese: ああ‥‥。 ゆうべの１０時ごろだったかな。
English: Ahh... Let's see... It must've taken place around 10 PM last night.

Japanese: 練習が終わってさ。つかれたから すぐ、ここにもどってきたんだ。
English: After rehearsals were finished, I was tuckered out, so I came back here.

Japanese: でね。ベッドに入ったとき ‥‥見ちまったんだよ。
English: After I went to bed... That's when I caught a peek of it...

Japanese: “見ちまった”‥‥？
English: "Caught a peek...?"

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。事件をね。
English: Of the crime.

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥）
English: (So this is what Detective Gumshoe was talking about...)

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事が言ってた “目撃者”‥‥この人だ！）
English: (Moe was the eyewitness to the crime!)

Japanese: 団長はエラいヒトだよ。 いつも新しいモノを取り入れてさ。
English: The Ringmaster was truly ahead of his time. He would always add new elements to the show.

Japanese: 新しいモノ‥‥。
English: New elements...?

Japanese: 長く芸人やってるとね。 どうしても芸が古くなるんだ。
English: When you've been a performer for a long time, your act starts getting a bit stale.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: オレっちもさ。 ウスウスわかってるんだ。
English: I realize that even my act can get a bit long in the tooth...

Japanese: 自分のギャグセンス、思ってる ほどナウくない、って。
English: Sometimes my jokes can be a bit... Umm... Old-fashion.

Japanese: “ウスウス”‥‥ですか。
English: "A bit long in the tooth?"

Japanese: でもさ！ あのワカゾーはヒドすぎるよ！
English: But that make-believer takes things too far!!

Japanese: ワカゾー‥‥？
English: Make-believer?

Japanese: あの手品師だよ！ マッコイとかいう野郎だ！
English: The magician!! The one that thinks he's all high and mighty!!

Japanese: 『キミはある種、オモシロイね』 ‥‥だとよ！
English: He had the gall to say to me, "You're one of those funny types, right?"

Japanese: なんだよ、“ある種”って！ ジョウダンはヨシコさん、だぜ！
English: What does he mean "one of those"!? Well the joke's on him now...

Japanese: ヨシコさん‥‥
English: On him...?

Japanese: とにかく！　みんな、アイツには ハラ立ててたんだよ！
English: Yep! He got on everyone's nerves!!

Japanese: 事件のあった日だって‥‥
English: The day of the murder...

Japanese: なんですか？
English: Go ahead.

Japanese: イヤ、いいや！ 忘れてくれよ。
English: Nope. No way! Just forget I said anything!

Japanese: （‥‥マックスのこと、 まだ何かかくしてるのかな‥‥）
English: (I bet he's still hiding something about Max...)

Japanese: 目撃したこと‥‥ 聞かせてもらえませんか？
English: You say you saw the crime. Do you mind telling me what you saw?

Japanese: いや、それがさ。ケーサツの ダンナに止められてるんだよ。
English: Well, the police told me that I can't share my story with others.

Japanese: “言ったらダメッスよ”って。 ‥‥だから、ゆるしてちゃぶ台！
English: "Don't say a word!!" I'm just gonna have to let these lips stay zipped!

Japanese: そこをなんとか！
English: That's not fair!

Japanese: そおだなあ‥‥。 聞かせてやってもいいぜ。
English: I guess you're right... Maybe I can tell you a few details.

Japanese: ‥‥そこのニイちゃんが、 おもしろいコト、言えたらな。
English: But only if you can get old stiff lips here to make with the funny...!

Japanese: え。
English: Stiff lips?
Wait... Do you mean me?

Japanese: なるほどくん！　がんばって！
English: Nick! You can do it!!

Japanese: え、え。
English: *achem* *achem*

Japanese: ホラ。どした？
English: What's the matter?

Japanese: ‥‥エヘン。
English: Just getting ready. OK... Do you know why I, Phoenix Wright, am a great lawyer?

Japanese: ‥‥へ、ヘタなシャレは、 やめ、やめなシャレ。
English: Because I'm Wright all the time!!

Japanese: なんでそうなるかなあ。
English: At least his expectations are low.

Japanese: それじゃカネは取れねえな。
English: I wouldn't let him quit his day job.

Japanese: （‥‥今すぐ死にたい‥‥）
English: (Geesh, cut a guy some slack. At least it was funnier than Chuckles over here.)

Japanese: ま、シロートにしちゃァ がんばった。聞かせてあげるよ。
English: It wasn't the greatest joke I've heard, but you did try, so I'll tell you what I saw.

Japanese: すみませんね。トミーさん。
English: I'm sorry he's incapable of being funny, Moe.

Japanese: ‥‥あの晩‥‥
English: That night...

Japanese: フトンに入ったとたん、 ものすごい音がしたんだ。
English: Once I had tucked myself into bed, I heard this amazing noise. It was incredibly loud.

Japanese: “ドカッ！”‥‥って。
English: It sounded like a giant "THUMP"!

Japanese: それで、あわてて ベッドから出て、そとを見ると‥‥
English: Once I heard it, I jumped out of bed. That's when I saw...

Japanese: ありゃあ、マッコイだったよ。 まちがいなく。
English: Without question. Without a doubt. It was that magician!!

Japanese: ‥‥ま。そういうこった。 ヤッパリ、ヒデェ男だわな。
English: That's all I saw... But it just proves how terrible that man actually is!

Japanese: （‥‥マックスについて、 まだ何か知っていそうだな‥‥）
English: (He knows more about Max than he is letting on...)

Japanese: あのキザなヤローの キザなボーシか。
English: It's that uppity faker's uppity top hat!

Japanese: 殺人現場に 落ちてたんだってなあ？
English: They found this at the scene of the crime, didn't they?

Japanese: そ、そうみたいですね。
English: That's what I've been told...

Japanese: くそ！　やっぱり アイツが団長を‥‥！
English: Unbelievable. That jerk killed the Ringmaster!

Japanese: 団長さんのことになると、 コワいねトミーさん。
English: It really is a shame what happened to the Ringmaster, huh Moe?

Japanese: ‥‥それだけ、いい人 だったんだよ。団長さん。
English: It seems that the Ringmaster was truly a great man...

Japanese: ‥‥団長！
English: RUSSELL!!

Japanese: こんなことに なっちまって‥‥
English: How could anyone do this to you...

Japanese: オレは‥‥オレはぁぁぁ！ I... I...
English: *sobs*

Japanese: トミーさん‥‥ かわいそう。
English: Moe's taking this really hard...

Japanese: これなんですけど‥‥ どうですか？
English: What do you think about this? Anything come to mind?

Japanese: お。プロのコメントを 聞きたいってか？
English: Ahh... You want some advice on your development as a comedian, right?

Japanese: そうだなー。じゃ、 こうしよう。
English: I see... Well... This is what you do...

Japanese: ニイちゃんが何か おもしろいコトを言ったら‥‥
English: When you tell a joke, imagine everyone's wearing underwear and dancing the lambada...

Japanese: やっぱり、イイです。
English: Umm... I think that's enough advice for now...

Japanese: なるほどくん！ いくじなしッ！
English: Nick! He was giving you good advice! Don't be so close-minded!!

Japanese: こいつとは、サーカスを 始める前からのつきあいだよ。
English: I've been friends with Russell since even before the circus began.

Japanese: 自分のことより、いっつも なかまのことばかり考えていた。
English: He was so selfless. He always thought about others before thinking of himself.

Japanese: 恩を返すつもりがさ。 いつも世話になりっぱなし。
English: I'll find a way to return the favor... He always took such good care of me.

Japanese: ヒトコト“ありがとう”って 言っておけばよかったよ‥‥。
English: I wish I would have thanked him more than I did...

Japanese: 団長のムスメさんだ。
English: That's the Ringmaster's daughter, Regina.

Japanese: 生まれたころから ずーっと見てきてる。
English: Ever since she was a little girl, she's been watching the circus performances.

Japanese: カワイイよなあ。 ウチのトモコとちがってさあ。
English: Cuter than a little puppy Maltese, that Regina. If only my Larina was that cute...

Japanese: トモコちゃん？
English: Larina?

Japanese: いや、オレっちのムスメ。
English: Ah... Larina is my daughter.

Japanese: かあちゃんといっしょに 出て行っちまったけど。
English: She lives with her mother now.

Japanese: （さすがピエロ‥‥。 ホロ苦い人生を歩んでるな）
English: (It's true what they say about the tears of a clown...)

Japanese: フン。団長が死ぬくらいなら、 かわりに‥‥
English: Hmph! If he thinks he can kill the Ringmaster, it's only just that he should die too!

Japanese: トミーさん！
English: Moe!!

Japanese: コリャさすがにマズかったな。 でもよ。ヒデェ男だよホント。
English: Sorry. I crossed the line. But he truly is a disgusting human being.

Japanese: 何かあったんですか？
English: Why do you hate him so much?

Japanese: このハナシ ‥‥言っちゃうか！
English: Let me tell you this one story...

Japanese: いやさ。事件があった朝もな、 タイヘンなコトがあったんだ。
English: The morning before the murder, something terrible happened.

Japanese: ベンのアタマをな。 思いっきり殴ったんだよ。
English: Max clonked Ben right over the head, as hard as he could.

Japanese: ベンさん‥‥？ （あの、無口な腹話術師か‥‥）
English: Ben? (The ventriloquist with the speech impediment?)

Japanese: 食堂に行ってみな。
English: You should go to the cafeteria and investigate for yourselves.

Japanese: 食堂‥‥？
English: The cafeteria?

Japanese: おもしろいモノがアル・カポネ。 ‥‥なーんて。
English: Let's just say there's Gotti be something interesting there...

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃひゃひゃ。 あひゃあひゃひゃ！
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: お。ベンちゃん。どうかした？
English: Ah, Ben... How's he doing?

Japanese: 腹話術師なんですよね？
English: He's a ventriloquist isn't he?

Japanese: そうよ。‥‥なんたって、
English: That's right! Boy was I surprised when they told me his secret!

Japanese: ヘソのかわりに口がついてるん だから、ビックリ。‥‥なーんて。
English: He's got a second mouth where his belly button should be!!

Japanese: あひゃ。ひゃひゃひゃあひゃ。 ひゃひゃひゃあひゃあひゃ！
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Just kidding...

Japanese: ベンさん、ゼンゼン教えて くれないんですよ、事件のこと。
English: Ben wouldn't tell me a single thing about the murder.

Japanese: ああ。そりゃアレだ。 リロがいないから。
English: Ahh... That's a simple problem to solve. He won't talk if he doesn't have his puppet Trilo!
Trilo...?

Japanese: アイツね。腹話術の人形がないと、 なんもしゃべれないんだよ。
English: If Ben doesn't have his ventriloquist's puppet, you'll barely get a word outta him.

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか。
English: Really?

Japanese: （腹話術の人形、ね‥‥）
English: (Hmm... Master and puppet...)
Ah!

Japanese: どうしたんですか？
English: What's the matter?

Japanese: なんか、自分のカオ見ると、 ギャグが出て来なくなるんだ。
English: Whenever I see my own nose, I am reminded of my Italian heritage...

Japanese: ははあ。
English: Ahhhh... I see...

Japanese: それってやっぱり、“シャレに ならないカオ”ってコトですか？
English: It must be because you have one of those "Roman Noses"... Roamin' all over your face.

Japanese: なな、なんかマズいコト 言っちゃったかな？
English: I didn't cross the line there did I?

Japanese: 言っちゃったんじゃないか？ やっぱり。
English: You went so far past it, you'll need a map to get back!

Japanese: いやー、すまないなあ。
English: This isn't very funny...

Japanese: サーカスの連中以外のコト、 なーんにも知らんのよオレっち。
English: How am I supposed to know about people who aren't in the circus!?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 宿舎１階　トミーの部屋
English: December 28 Lodging Hall 1st Floor Moe's Room

Japanese: おッ！　“ヘタなシャレは、 やめなシャレ”のニイちゃん！
English: Oh my! If it isn't "Mr. Wright All The Time"!!

Japanese: うッ！
English: *ugh*

Japanese: いやー、“やめなシャレ”は よかったなあ、ニイちゃん！
English: It's all Wright to be wrong every now and again, Wright?

Japanese: よかったね、ニイちゃん！
English: See Nick! It just took awhile for the joke to find its audience!

Japanese: うううううう。
English: *sigh*

Japanese: どした？　今度はウマいシャレでも 思いついたかい？
English: So what can I do for you? Did you remember a good joke you wanted to tell me?

Japanese: ま、そこのナイスなイスに座って、 ゆっくり聞かせてよ。
English: Pull up a chair, or maybe just pull my finger, and let me have it.

Japanese: あ。“ナイスなイス”って、 もしかして‥‥？
English: We're going to get the same sound effect either way, aren't we?

Japanese: お。気がついた？　気がついた？ もー、さすがおぢょーさん！
English: How'd you know I put a whopee cushion on the chair!?

Japanese: わかってるねー。 “笑い”のなんたるか！
English: You really know what it takes to be a clown, don't you?

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃあひゃ。 あひゃひゃひゃあひゃひゃ！
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: ミリカちゃん‥‥、 変わってますよね。
English: So, about Regina...

Japanese: ミリカか。‥‥イイ子だよ。 ピュアな、ね。
English: Regina is such a pure, innocent child...

Japanese: ぴゅあ‥‥ですかあ。
English: She's such a cutie, too.

Japanese: 彼女、このサーカスで生まれて、 このサーカスで育ったんだ。
English: She was born and raised in the circus you know?

Japanese: そとの世界を、まるで 知らないんだよ。
English: But that means she doesn't really know much about the world outside the Big Top.

Japanese: はみちゃんといっしょだ‥‥
English: Sounds like Pearly...

Japanese: 彼女にとっては、サーカスという “夢”が、そのまんま現実なんだ。
English: For her, every child's "dream" of the circus is her everyday reality.

Japanese: “夢”の世界‥‥。
English: She lives in a dream world...

Japanese: 猛獣のダンス、空を飛ぶ魔術師、 ひょうきんなピエロ‥‥。
English: Everyday she sees dancing wild animals, men flying in the air, and one very funny clown.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、 彼女の現実のすべてなのさ。
English: The funny thing is, that all seems normal to her because it is her everyday life.

Japanese: （腹話術の人形とケッコンするのも 現実なのか‥‥？）
English: (I guess that explains why she thinks she can marry a ventriloquist's puppet...)

Japanese: いいコトか悪いコトかなんて、 聞くなよ。
English: Don't ask me if her reality is a good thing or a bad thing though...

Japanese: ピエロにはそんなの、 わかんないから。
English: "A clown sees life simply, without complications."

Japanese: あの。ルーサーって知ってますか？
English: Have you ever heard of a monkey named Money?

Japanese: ああ。あのサル。
English: Ahhh! Yes! Money!

Japanese: ぼくの弁護士バッジ、 取られちゃったんですよ。
English: He stole my Attorney's Badge!

Japanese: あー。ヤッコさん、ピカピカ光る モノが大好きだからなあ。
English: Well... Money does love shiny objects... It makes sense that he'd swipe your badge.

Japanese: あ。でもな、ゼッタイ 追いかけちゃダメだぞ。
English: But under no circumstances can you chase after him.

Japanese: え。そうなんですか？
English: Eh? Why's that?

Japanese: あ！　わかった！ “サルもの、追わず”ですね！
English: Oh! I know!! You don't want to get involved in any monkey business, right!?

Japanese: ピンポーン！　ブラボー おぢょーさん！　ブラボー！
English: EXACTLY! BRAVO!! BRAVO!!

Japanese: ま。それはいいとして。
English: Enough joking around though...

Japanese: あのな。ルーサーは、ミリカの ファミリーじゃないんだよ。
English: Money isn't considered a member of Regina's family.

Japanese: ‥‥どなたの ふぁみりーなんですか？
English: Then who does he belong to?

Japanese: ‥‥そうだな。 連れてってやるよ。部屋に。
English: I'll be happy to take you to where his owner is staying...

Japanese: え。これからですか？
English: You mean right now?

Japanese: モチ。行くだろ？
English: Of course. Shall we go?

Japanese: （他に、トミーさんに 聞いておくことはないかな？）
English: (Hmm, should we go with him now, or wait a while?)

Japanese: もう少し お話を聞いてからにします。
English: We'll go after we talk some more, OK?

Japanese: おッ。オレっちのハナシを 聞きたいって！
English: Yay! You want to hear me talk some more, huh!?

Japanese: よおし。じゃあ、おなじみ トミーのギャグ１０００連発ッ！
English: Your wish is my command!! I'll give ya the full repertoire of 1001 jokes!

Japanese: よッ！
English: Woohoo!!

Japanese: “よッ”じゃないよ！
English: Don't "Woohoo!!" that!!

Japanese: じゃあ、おねがいします。
English: Sure... Lead the way, Moe!

Japanese: あのサルな。３階の、 アクロのヤツが世話してるんだ。
English: That monkey is taking care of Acro in his room on the 3rd floor.

Japanese: でも、今日はアイツ、 ルスだったなたしか‥‥。
English: But I hear that Acro's not around today...

Japanese: お。ベンちゃん。どうかした？
English: This is Ben. Is something wrong?

Japanese: 腹話術師なんですよね？
English: He's a ventriloquist, right?

Japanese: そうよ。‥‥なんたって、
English: That's correct.

Japanese: ヘソのかわりに口がついてるん だから、ビックリ。‥‥なーんて。
English: Boy was I surprised to find out he's got a mouth for a belly button!

Japanese: あひゃ。ひゃひゃひゃあひゃ。 ひゃひゃひゃあひゃあひゃ！
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。トミーさん、 どこか行っちゃったね。
English: Huh? Moe must have gone off somewhere...

Japanese: ふう‥‥やれやれ。
English: Just as long as he isn't off writing new jokes...

Japanese: あ。そうそう、 なるほどくん。
English: Oh, that reminds me Nick...

Japanese: 弁護士バッジ、明日までに みつけないとマズいよ？
English: You have to get your Attorney's Badge back by tomorrow, or we're in trouble.

Japanese: そ、そうだったね。
English: I know. I know.

Japanese: （あのサルのねぐらを テッテー的に探さないと‥‥）
English: (I'm gonna have to try and find out where that monkey hid it...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 宿舎３階　アクロの部屋
English: December 28 Lodging Hall 3rd Floor Acro's Room

Japanese: はあ、はあ‥‥。
English: *huff* *huff*

Japanese: ここだ。 ‥‥どうした？　ニイちゃん。
English: This is it... What's wrong, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: い‥‥イキが‥‥。
English: I... can't... breathe...

Japanese: なさけないねー。３階まで、 階段をのぼっただけなのに。
English: Don't be such a wimp! You only had to climb two flights of stairs!!

Japanese: うう‥‥。
English: *pant* *pant*

Japanese: とにかく、ここだ。 ‥‥アクロの部屋。
English: Anyways... This is the place. Acro's room.

Japanese: アクロ‥‥？
English: Acro?

Japanese: アクロバットの芸人だ。 今日はちょっと、いないけどな。
English: He's an acrobat... It seems like he's not around today...

Japanese: あっ。あそこ。‥‥なんだか、 ガラクタがいっぱいあるよ！
English: Eh... That's a big pile of junk over in the corner...

Japanese: ニイちゃんのガラクタも、 きっとあそこにあるよ。
English: I don't think it'd be wrong to assume that Phoenix's stuff is over there too...

Japanese: ‥‥んじゃあな！
English: Just be careful to make sure you've got the Wright stuff.

Japanese: さよなら、トミーさん。
English: Thanks Moe! See you later!

Japanese: 証拠品《ルーサー》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Money has been added to the Court Record.

Japanese: うわあ‥‥。
English: Holy cow!

Japanese: フォークやらカガミやら、 光るモノがいっぱい！
English: There's a fork... And a mirror... Everything's shiny!!

Japanese: ヤケに安っぽいウデ時計も あるし‥‥
English: There's even a really cheap looking knockoff wristwatch!

Japanese: これは‥‥トロフィーか。 けっこう重いものもあるぞ。
English: Look at this! It's a trophy! And it's really heavy!!

Japanese: あっ！　なるほどくん、 あったよ！　バッジ。
English: Nick! I found it! Your badge! It's right here!!

Japanese: 《弁護士バッジ》を エリにもどした。
English: Phoenix's Attorney's Badge has been returned.

Japanese: ありがとう、助かったよ。 ‥‥‥ん？
English: Thanks! You really saved me! Huh?

Japanese: どうかした？
English: What's the matter? Did you find something?

Japanese: これは‥‥。
English: Yeah. Check this out.

Japanese: ゆびわ‥‥だね。
English: It's a ring...

Japanese: なにか、刻みこまれてるぞ。 ‥‥“ＲからＭへ”か‥‥。
English: There's something engraved on it... "From T to R"...

Japanese: 証拠品《ゆびわ》を ポケットにしまった。
English: Ring put in pocket.

Japanese: ‥‥さてと。そろそろ 調査は切り上げようか。
English: Well... I think it's about time we wrap up our investigation...

Japanese: 勝てる？　明日の法廷。
English: Do you think we'll win in court tomorrow?

Japanese: どんな証言が出てくるのか 想像がつかないからなあ。
English: Who knows... Even I can't imagine what kind of testimony will come out tomorrow.

Japanese: （明日の証人は‥‥ やっぱり、トミーさんか？）
English: (I'm guessing Moe will be a witness in court tomorrow...)

Japanese: （それとも、あの人形か‥‥）
English: (Moe and maybe the puppet.)

Japanese: だいじょうぶ！　こっちには 魔術師がついてるからね！
English: Don't worry Nick. No matter what, we've still got a magician on our side!!

Japanese: ‥‥だといいけど。
English: That's good, because we might need some magic tomorrow...

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: あ、見て！　見て！ マックスだよ。
English: Look! Look! It's Max!!

Japanese: ‥‥見る気がなくても 目に入ってくるよ。
English: Even when you don't want to see him... *poof* He's right in front of you.

Japanese: （“タチミ・サーカス”とは 書いてあるけど‥‥）
English: (Sure the sign says "Berry Big Circus"...)

Japanese: （どう見てもマックスひとりの カンバンだよな）
English: (But looking around... It might as well be Cirque du Galactica...)

Japanese: あのホッペのホシが シビれるよねー。
English: The stars on his cheek sure are dreamy...

Japanese: なるほどくんにも書いて あげようか？　サインペンで。
English: How about I draw a star on your cheek Nick? I've got a marker!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくはいいや。
English: Nah! Nah! It's alright!

Japanese: チケットを売っているボックスだ。 サムいのに大変だな。
English: This is the Box Office where they sell all the tickets for the circus.

Japanese: あ。そういえば、こないだ パンフレット買い忘れちゃった。
English: They also sell programs. I forgot to buy one when we came to the circus last time.

Japanese: じゃあ、買ってくれば。今。
English: So then why don't you buy one now?

Japanese: そうしようかなあ。
English: Hmm... Sounds like a plan.

Japanese: ‥‥あ。今日はおサイフ 忘れてきちゃった。
English: Oh no... It looks like I forgot my wallet.

Japanese: スナオに“買って”って 言えよ！
English: If you want me to buy it for you, just ask me already.

Japanese: いやー、わるいね なるほどくん。
English: You know I'd never do that to you, Nick...

Japanese: ん。このドアはナニかな？
English: Hmm... This door must lead to the lodging house.

Japanese: “関係者以外立入禁止”って 書いてあるぞ。
English: "No Entry To Unauthorized Personnel"

Japanese: “立入禁止”って書くだけで ダレも立ち入らなかったら、
English: Do you really need to say "No Entry" if no one's actually entering?

Japanese: ケーサツはいらないよ なるほどくん。
English: It's almost like a zen riddle, isn't it Nick?

Japanese: （うーん。少し説得力があるな）
English: (I'm not even going to justify that question with a response...)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやらこのドアの先には 芸人さんの宿舎があるみたいだね。
English: I bet all of the stars stay at that lodging house.

Japanese: 売店だ。ホットドッグに ニクまん、あんまん、
English: It's a snack stand. They have hot dogs, hamburgers, and drinks... Not to mention...

Japanese: おしるこ、たこやきに‥‥ “冷たいかきごおり”だって！
English: Candy and popcorn... They've even got snow cones!!

Japanese: （な、なんでこの季節に かきごおりが‥‥）
English: (Who would eat snow cones in the middle of winter?)

Japanese: なるほどくん。 かきごおり食べたい。
English: Nick... Do you think we can buy some snow cones?

Japanese: ‥‥そのへんに いくらでもつもってるだろ。
English: Look around... There's tons of snow piled up all around here!

Japanese: わーい。‥‥って、 シロップかかってないじゃない！
English: YAY!! Wait a second... There's no syrup though! I want syrup!

Japanese: （かかってりゃ、いいのか‥‥？）
English: (Hopefully she doesn't notice that discolored snow in the corner. That's not syrup!)

Japanese: さ。さ。入り口はこっちだよ なるほどくん。
English: Nick... The entrance is right here.

Japanese: 今日はやってないぞ。 サーカス。
English: Maya. The circus is closed today. No clowns. No elephants. No shows.

Japanese: わかってるよ！
English: I know that!

Japanese: ホラ、入り口の横で、ゾウの ヒロミくんと写真が撮れるんだよ！
English: Nick... You can get your picture taken with Dali the elephant!

Japanese: 今日はいないぞ。 ヒロミくん。
English: There's no Dali. Not today.

Japanese: わかってるよ！
English: I know that too!

Japanese: ‥‥しょうがないなー。 おまわりさんと撮ろうか。写真。
English: Oh well... I'll just have to take a picture with whoever I stumble across...

Japanese: （ナニしにきたんだ‥‥）
English: (*sigh* It's not like we're here on business or anything...)

Japanese: おっきいねー、 サーカスのテントって。
English: The Berry Big Top is so... Very... B... *cough* Enormous...

Japanese: そうだなあ。コイツを見ると サーカスに来た、って感じがする。
English: Yep. One look at the huge tent looming over you and you realize "This is the circus!"

Japanese: うん！　わくわくするよね。
English: I know! I know! It really gets your blood pumping doesn't it!?

Japanese: サーカスのアドバルーンだ。
English: Those streamers do a nice job introducing the circus performers.

Japanese: “マックス・ギャラクティカと ゆかいな仲間たち”だって。
English: "Maximillion Galactica and his comical comrades!"

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば。
English: You know...

Japanese: アドバルーンて、あんまり 見かけないね。最近。
English: You don't really see too many streamers nowadays, do you?

Japanese: そうだねえ。
English: You're right... I haven't seen one in ages.

Japanese: 子どもがよじ登ったら あぶないからね。
English: I bet they stopped using them due to little kids climbing up to the top...

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんなら やりかねないな‥‥）
English: (Umm... I don't think that was why...)

Japanese: なんだろ。写真が並んでるよ、 ズラリと。
English: Nick... Look at all the photos lined up on the wall...

Japanese: このサーカスのレキシ‥‥ みたいなものらしいね。
English: It's like a guided tour of the circus' history.

Japanese: へええ。いいね、なんか 思い出がいっぱい、ってカンジで。
English: This is so cool! It seems like there were so many happy memories.

Japanese: そうだ！　あたしたちの事務所 でもやろうよ、それ。
English: Maybe we should do this sort of thing at our office?

Japanese: 無罪になった依頼人のヒトたちの 写真、じゃんじゃんかざるの。
English: We can put up pictures of all the clients who have been found not guilty.

Japanese: 有罪になった依頼人は？
English: What about the clients who were found guilty?

Japanese: うーん。エンギわるいから、 ハズしとこうか。
English: Umm... We'll just pretend like they don't exist. How's that?

Japanese: ダメだよ！　負けるコトなんて 考えちゃあ！
English: Nick! Now you've got me thinking about losing cases! Why'd you do that!?

Japanese: なんか、おんなじような ガクブチがならんでるよ。
English: All of these frames look the same.

Japanese: カンシャ状‥‥みたいだ。
English: They almost look like... Thank You cards.

Japanese: 団長さん、毎年、寄付を していたみたいだよ。
English: It looks like every year the Ringmaster made donations to charity...

Japanese: ‥‥ロボット・ピエロの 研究グループに。
English: To the Robot Clown Research Center...

Japanese: ダマされてたんじゃない？
English: You're kidding right?

Japanese: そ、そういうコト言っちゃ いけないと思うな。
English: What!? They may be a perfectly reputable charity in the field of advanced tomfoolery!

Japanese: 団長さん、ここで お化粧してたんだね。
English: This is where the Ringmaster applied his makeup.

Japanese: 化粧品のビンがならんでいる。 ハデなのばっかりだ。
English: It's quite a collection of the most understated colors. Shocking Pink for example.

Japanese: “１００パーセント天然素材の マスカラ”って書いてあるよ。
English: This one says it is "100% All Natural Organic Mascara."

Japanese: こっちも、“おハダにやさしい ドーラン”だって。
English: And this one says "Sensitive enough for a baby, strong enough for a mime."

Japanese: ‥‥いろいろ気をつかうヒト だったみたいだね。
English: The Ringmaster must have been really concerned about skin care... Very metrosexual.

Japanese: ‥‥イガイに安っぽいね。 団長さんのデスク。
English: This is strange... Everything else looks nice, but this desk looks old and cheap.

Japanese: 大きな写真立てがあるぞ。 写ってるのは‥‥
English: There's a really big photo on the desk...

Japanese: 団長さんと、ミリカちゃん ‥‥か。
English: It's a picture of Regina and her father, the Ringmaster.

Japanese: 団長さん、ホントにかわいがって たんだ。ミリカちゃんのコト。
English: He really loved her didn't he? Regina was lucky to have such a wonderful father.

Japanese: わあ。かわいいトロフィーが いっぱいあるよ。
English: Nick! Look at all the cute trophies!!

Japanese: すごいね。いろんな賞を とってるんだ、このサーカス。
English: Indeed. Just look at all the awards this circus has won...

Japanese: なになに。
English: Like...

Japanese: “町内クイズ王” “団長杯ゴルフコンペ”
English: "All County Quiz Champions"... "Ringmaster's Association Mini Golf Master"...

Japanese: “大食い選手権優勝” “ペット自慢グランプリ”
English: "Beer Belly Balloon Bounce Champ"... "Pet Grooming Grand Prix"...

Japanese: ‥‥こぢんまりした ヒトだったんだね。
English: Wow. The Ringmaster was multi-talented in ways I could have never imagined...

Japanese: 来客用のテーブルだ。
English: This was the table he used when visitors came to see him.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ドロが こびりついてるよ。テーブルの上。
English: Eww... There's mud caked on the table.

Japanese: ぎょうぎの悪い客が クツのまま足を乗せたんだろうね。
English: Someone with terrible manners must have put their shoes up on the table!

Japanese: ダメだよ。なるほどくんは そんなコトしちゃ。
English: Nick! Don't even think about it!

Japanese: しないよ！
English: I wasn't! How uncouth do you think I am anyway?

Japanese: みんなサーカスのポスターだ。
English: Look at all the stars on this poster...

Japanese: 今までの公演で 使ってきたものだろう。
English: This must have been the poster they used to promote their public appearances.

Japanese: うーん。やっぱりこれからは ポスターだね。
English: Posters are the way to go aren't they?

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: ウチの事務所も作ろうよ！ ポスター。
English: We should make posters to promote our law firm.

Japanese: “手に汗ニギるスリルと サスペンス！　そして恐怖！”
English: "Spine tingling legal action! Mind numbing legalese! You will say 'wow'!"

Japanese: “絶体絶命！　イノチをかけた 法廷ショー”‥‥とか。どう？
English: Or perhaps, "Hold it! Don't miss out on a stunning life or death courtroom thrill ride!"

Japanese: 依頼人、来なくなるぞ。
English: With those taglines, our law firm would sink faster than the Titanic...

Japanese: ヨゴれた食器が つみ上げられている。
English: There are dirty dishes all over the place...

Japanese: かたづけるのが メンドウなのだろう。
English: It must have been too hectic last night to clean up.

Japanese: “メンドウなのだろう” じゃないよ！　なるほどくん！
English: You know I can't stand a mess Nick!

Japanese: ホラ。あたしたちで かたづけようよ！
English: I think you and I should clean this place up for them!

Japanese: なな、なんで ぼくたちが‥‥！ up...!?
English: W-Why do we have to clean

Japanese: きたない食堂は ユルせないもん、あたし。
English: One, because I hate dirty cafeterias. Two, because one bears repeating.

Japanese: い、いやホラ。 殺人事件があったわけだから。
English: B-But... This is a crime scene!

Japanese: 現場はキチンと保存 しておかないと。
English: We can't clean it up! We have to preserve it for evidence!!

Japanese: うう‥‥。 気になるなあ、もう。
English: Arrrggghh... You know I really hate dirty cafeterias!!

Japanese: （やれやれ。ぼくも かたづけはニガテだからな‥‥）
English: (Not as much as I hate cleaning dirty cafeterias!)

Japanese: 事件があった日のまま、 ってカンジだね。
English: It looks like they've left it exactly as it was on the night of the murder.

Japanese: それにしても ちらかってるよねえ。
English: They didn't seem too worried about cleaning up the dishes, did they?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだか、おなか すいてきちゃったよ。
English: Hmm... Looking at all these dishes is making me hungry!

Japanese: ソトの売店で たこやき食べようか。
English: Let's go get a burger at the snack stand outside.

Japanese: 事件がカイケツしたらね。
English: Once we're finished with the case.

Japanese: よし！　いっちょ、やるよ！ なるほどくん！
English: Alright! Then let's get this over with Nick!! Here we go!! Whoahh!

Japanese: （キアイじゅうぶんだ‥‥）
English: (Where's the mute button when you need it?)

Japanese: 伝言板だ。 ‥‥連絡用かな。
English: Ah, a bulletin board... For... Umm... Bulletins.

Japanese: 特に手がかりになるような ことは書いてないな。
English: It doesn't look like there are any useful clues posted here.

Japanese: つまんないなー。 ‥‥なんか書いておこうか？
English: Boring... Maybe we should leave a juicy tidbit for someone to read!

Japanese: え。ナニを？
English: Juicy?

Japanese: ニセの手がかり。‥‥そうだなあ。 たとえば“犯行声明”とか。
English: You know... Like a fake clue! Hmm... Maybe something like... "Message from the Killer!"

Japanese: やめとこう。イトノコ刑事が ホンキにするぞ。
English: Give it up Maya... You know Gumshoe would take it all seriously.

Japanese: イスが倒れてる。
English: The chair's been knocked over.

Japanese: 見たまんまだね。
English: That's what it looks like.

Japanese: どうも、だれかが 暴れたみたいに見えるな。
English: It almost looks as if someone knocked it over in a struggle, or used it for self defense...

Japanese: ラトーがごはん食べたんじゃ ないかな。ここで。
English: Or maybe Regent just likes to eat his dinner in the cafeteria?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろ？　この台。 何も乗ってないけど。
English: This is strange... There's nothing on top of this stand.

Japanese: “マックス”って書いてあるよ。 専用の席かな。
English: Look here... Max is written on it. It must be his VIP table.

Japanese: ‥‥さすがに、ちょっと せまいんじゃないか？
English: Isn't a bit small to be a VIP table? You won't be putting a ten course meal on this.

Japanese: ラーメンぐらいなら 食べられそうだけどね。
English: Well, he could still eat hamburgers, right?

Japanese: 『食堂内は清潔に！』 って書いてあるよ。
English: "Keep Our Cafeteria Clean!"

Japanese: そのワリに、きたないな。
English: Doesn't seem like anyone ever read this sign, huh?

Japanese: “せいけつに”って、 ひらがなで書いたらいいのにね。
English: Maybe they should make it easier to understand... "Clean it or die!"

Japanese: 怒られるぞ、 ここのサーカスのヒトに。
English: Well, that would definitely make them clean up!

Japanese: この奥はキッチンに なってるみたいだ。
English: The kitchen is over here.

Japanese: 食事係は当番制なのかな。 リストが張ってある。
English: Everyone must have taken turns cooking. There's a duty list posted here.

Japanese: あたしもやってみたいな。 食事当番。
English: I wouldn't mind trying my hand at cooking for this many people.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんの必殺ラーメン。 ‥‥食べてみたいでしょ？
English: Maya's Killer Hamburger... You'd try it, wouldn't you?

Japanese: うわー、大きなライト。
English: Whoa! Those lights are huge!

Japanese: ミリカ、ライト大好き。
English: I love lights!!

Japanese: ミリカにライトが集まると、 みんな拍手してくれるの。
English: Whenever I appear under the spotlight, everyone claps for me!

Japanese: だってミリカちゃん、 カワイイもんねー。
English: That's because everyone knows that you're cute!

Japanese: そんなコトないよ。 真宵ちゃんだって。
English: No... I'm not cute. You're cute!

Japanese: えッ！
English: Me!?

Japanese: きっと、なれるよ。 ヒロインに。
English: Of course! I'm sure you'd make an incredible heroine!

Japanese: きゃあ！　ホント！
English: Really!? You think so!?

Japanese: ね。ね。じゃあさ。 なるほどくんは。どう？
English: Nick! Did you hear that!? Me!! A heroine!! What about Nick, Regina?

Japanese: うーん。どうかなあ。
English: Hmm... Nick...

Japanese: ダメだって。
English: He's no hero, is he?

Japanese: （‥‥ちょっとキズついた）
English: (Ouch... Thanks a lot Maya!)

Japanese: あ、あれ！　あそこから 出てきたんだよね、マックス！
English: Look! That's where Max comes out during the show!!

Japanese: ああ、あれは カッコよかったな。
English: I've got to admit, that was a pretty cool effect!

Japanese: あのね。今度のステージ、 ミリカもあそこから出るんだよ。
English: We're planning for me to start coming out of the lion during the show.

Japanese: へえ。そいつはすごいね！
English: That's great Regina!

Japanese: うん。ラトーの背中に 乗って、飛び出すの。
English: Yeah. I will ride on Regent's back and jump out of the lion's mouth.

Japanese: ううう。あたしも やってみたいよー。
English: I wanna try it too!

Japanese: なるほどくんの背中に 乗って、飛び出すの。
English: I'll ride on Nick's back and jump out of the lion's mouth!

Japanese: （なんでぼくなんだよ！）
English: (Sometimes I wonder about this girl...)

Japanese: 客席、けっこう遠いね。
English: The seats are kinda far away don't you think?

Japanese: うん。でも、かなり ヒトが入れそうだな。
English: They are, but it also means that lots of people can fit in the big top.

Japanese: ５００人ぐらい 入るんだよ。お客さん。
English: He's right. We can fit 500 people into a show.

Japanese: へええ。
English: Five hundred... That's amazing.

Japanese: その上を飛びまわるの、 すごく気持ちいいんだって。
English: Flying around above that many people is a "real rush".

Japanese: マックスが言ってたよ。
English: At least that's what Max said.

Japanese: ‥‥特に手がかりになるような ものは、入ってない。
English: There doesn't seem to be anything here that can help us... Not a single clue.

Japanese: じゃあ聞くけどさ、 なるほどくん。
English: You know I've been meaning to ask you...

Japanese: “特に手がかりになるようなモノ” って、ナニ？
English: What exactly do you mean when you say "clue"? What are you looking for?

Japanese: 血まみれのナイフとか。
English: A bloody chainsaw for instance...

Japanese: うーん。そりゃま、 入ってないだろうねえ‥‥。
English: Well there's definitely not one of those here...

Japanese: あ。あそこにあるの、 ロープだよね！
English: Hey! It's a rope!

Japanese: うん。ツナわたりの ロープなのかな‥‥？
English: Probably for tightrope walking...

Japanese: おかしいなあ。
English: That's a bit strange...

Japanese: こないだのプログラム、 ツナわたりなんか、なかったよ。
English: There weren't any tightrope walkers in the show when we saw it.

Japanese: あ、ハシゴだ。
English: Ah, a ladder.

Japanese: それはキャタツだよ。
English: It's just a stepladder.

Japanese: どうちがうの？ 同じようなものじゃない。
English: What's the difference? They do the same thing, right?

Japanese: もっとホンシツを見ようよ、 なるほどくん。
English: I think you should stick with the basic facts of the matter.

Japanese: ははは。
English: Oh... Uh... OK.

Japanese: （‥‥どこかで、似たような 会話をかわしたような‥‥）
English: (It's not even worth arguing with her on this one.)

Japanese: うわー、大きなライト。
English: Whoa! Those lights are huge!

Japanese: 落ちてきたら死んじゃうね こりゃあ。
English: If they fell from the ceiling, you'd be going to the circus in the sky...

Japanese: 人生なんて、はかないものだねえ なるほどくん。
English: Life is so fragile, isn't it Nick?

Japanese: ま、まあね。
English: It can be snuffed out in an instant by falling gargantuan lights.

Japanese: 死ぬまでに、いっぱい おいしいもの食べとかないと。
English: It's so fragile that I've decided to eat as much good food as I can before I die.

Japanese: たこやきとか。
English: Like hamburgers...

Japanese: ソトの売店、 今日はやってないぞ。
English: Apparently you didn't notice the big sign that said "closed" by the snack stand.

Japanese: うう‥‥ザンネン。
English: Awww... It's closed!?

Japanese: あ、あれ！　あそこから 出てきたんだよね、マックス！
English: Hey! Look! That's where Max makes his grand appearance!

Japanese: ああ、あれは カッコよかったな。
English: It's "grand" alright.

Japanese: あたしも出てみたい。 あそこから。
English: I want to try entering court like that.

Japanese: （言うと思った‥‥）
English: (How'd I know that she'd say that...)

Japanese: 客席、けっこう遠いね。
English: The seats are so far away from the ring...

Japanese: うん。でも、かなり ヒトが入れそうだな。
English: But they make up for it with the amount of people that can see a show.

Japanese: ね。ね。なるほどくん。
English: Hey Nick...

Japanese: マンインのお客さんが いっせいに深呼吸したら、
English: Do you think that if every person in the audience screamed all at once,

Japanese: ペコッ、てヘコむかなあ。 ‥‥テント。
English: the vibration they'd create could cause the big top to collapse?

Japanese: あるいはね。
English: I'm glad I don't pay you to come up with this stuff...

Japanese: ここだけ、雪が けちらされてるね。
English: This is the only place that the snow has been trounced upon.

Japanese: うう‥‥ずいぶんハデに あらそったんだね、ハンニンたち。
English: The murderer was sloppy leaving all these prints all over!

Japanese: いやいやいや。けちらしたのは ワレワレッス。
English: No no no no no... We're the ones who left the prints...

Japanese: なにしろ、捜査したッスから。
English: An investigation can be a messy thing sometimes.

Japanese: なあんだ。
English: What!?

Japanese: それに、自分が そのへんでスッころんだッス。
English: I also slipped and fell in that spot over there.

Japanese: 鑑識のヒトたちには怒られ、 検事にはムチでたたかれ。
English: The other detectives all got a good laugh when the prosecutor whipped me.

Japanese: とってもイタかったッス。
English: Thank god there was all this snow around to bring down the swelling.

Japanese: （ノンキな刑事さんだなあ‥‥）
English: (It's great to know that the police aren't worried about preserving the evidence...)

Japanese: ‥‥シートでくるまれた 資材だね。
English: There's some evidence under the tarp over there...

Japanese: 気をつけるッス！
English: Hey! Watch it, pal!

Japanese: そこのカゲに、ハンニンが かくれてるかも！
English: The killer is behind that tarp!!

Japanese: きゃわわわああっ！
English: AHHHHH!!

Japanese: なーんて。 ジョークッスジョークッス。
English: Ho! Ho! Ho! Got ya! I was just kidding!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんキック！
English: GRRR!

Japanese: ハッハッハッ。
English: Ho! Ho! Ho! Ho! Ho!

Japanese: 今年こそ、 エアコンを当てるッス！
English: This year, I finally won an air conditioner!

Japanese: え。お部屋にないんですか？ エアコン。
English: What!? You didn't have an air conditioner? Did you ride your triceratops to work too?

Japanese: そもそも、なんですか？ “当てる”って。
English: And what do you mean you "won" an air conditioner? You didn't buy it?

Japanese: いやー。買うのはとても ムリッスから、
English: I can't afford one of those things!!

Japanese: 年末のクジ引きでイッパツ、 ドーンと。
English: But I got lucky and won it as a door prize at the Annual Police Christmas Party!

Japanese: 安いんですね。お給料。
English: They really pay you peanuts don't they?

Japanese: ‥‥どういうワケか、 音を立てて下がっていくッス。
English: Peanuts? I don't even get paid enough for peanut butter, let alone peanuts!

Japanese: この街灯は、夜は ずっとついているッス。
English: The safety lights around the circus are kept on all night long.

Japanese: おそらく、事件の瞬間を ライトアップしていたはずッス。
English: So, they should have been on at the time of the murder...

Japanese: （わざわざ明るいところで 事件が起こったのか‥‥？）
English: (So he's saying the murder took place in the light...?)

Japanese: ちょっぴり、 ミョーッスよねえ‥‥。
English: How strange...

Japanese: たしかに、ミョーッスねえ。
English: How strange indeed... Hmm...

Japanese: 団長さんの死体は、 ここで見つかった。
English: The Ringmaster's body was found here.

Japanese: 木箱をかかえるようにして 倒れていたらしいけど、
English: I heard that the way he fell over made him look like he was carrying a wooden box...

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、ここで 何があったんだろう。
English: But what in the world really happened here?

Japanese: シートでくるまれた資材だ。 古いカンバンなどが置かれている。
English: Something's covered up by this tarp... An old sign perhaps.

Japanese: 気をつけて！　なるほどくん！
English: Nick! Look out!

Japanese: そこのカゲに、ハンニンが かくれてるかも！
English: They've got the killer trapped behind that tarp!

Japanese: つまんないの。
English: What? It wasn't funny?

Japanese: ちょっとぐらい、おどろいて くれてもいいのにさ。
English: You know it wouldn't have hurt you to look even a little bit surprised.

Japanese: （‥‥じつは、ビックリして 声も出なかったんだけど）
English: (Whew! She didn't notice that I was too scared to talk!)

Japanese: エアコンの室外機だ。 しばらく使われていないようだ。
English: This is the condenser for the air conditioner. It hasn't been used in a while.

Japanese: ええっ！　どうして そんなコトわかるの！
English: What!? How'd you figure that out!? You're not making things up now are you!?

Japanese: そ、そんなにおどろかれても こまるけど。
English: Hold your horses! It was easy to figure out!

Japanese: 雪は、事件の前にやんでいただろ。
English: It was snowing before the murder, right?

Japanese: そのときつもった雪が、 まだとけていないから。
English: Well, look at the condenser. The snow is still piled on top of it.

Japanese: うおッ！　すごい！ なるほどくん、名探偵だッ！
English: Wow! Nick! I'm impressed. Maybe you should be a P.I. instead of an attorney.

Japanese: この街灯、事件のあったときも ついていたんだよね。
English: This safety light was on at the time of the murder, wasn't it?

Japanese: そうだなあ。
English: That's what they say.

Japanese: （宿舎には、芸人さんたちが いたんだから‥‥）
English: (There were performers in the lodging house, so it makes sense that it was on.)

Japanese: （だれかに目撃されるキケン性は じゅうぶんあったはずだ‥‥）
English: (I bet the killer was worried that someone would spot them during the crime...)

Japanese: うわあ。かわいいクツだよ！
English: Awesome! Look at these shoes! They're great!

Japanese: それより、ぼくは バナナの皮が気になるけどな。
English: Forget the shoes! Check out the great gag banana peel!

Japanese: どうして？　おやつでしょ？ ピエロさんの。
English: You sure it wasn't Moe's snack after lunch?

Japanese: （何度か踏まれた あとがあるんだよな、この皮）
English: (Are you blind? Look at how many scratches there are from people slipping on it!!)

Japanese: 見て！　見て！　ピエロ・グッズが たくさんあるよ！
English: Tee hee... Clown equipment is so funny looking!

Japanese: 玉乗りの玉に、一輪車、 トランポリンまである。
English: He's got a balancing ball, a unicycle... He's even got a trampoline!!

Japanese: ‥‥みんなコワれてるけど。
English: But they're all broken.

Japanese: 練習ネッシンなピエロさん なんだね、きっと。
English: Maybe he was just a little too excited during practice?

Japanese: （何かひとつでも、 モノになってるのだろうか？）
English: (Who knows with that guy. Maybe that's part of the gag?)

Japanese: なんだか、むずかしそうな 本がつんである。
English: Moe seems to be a voracious reader. Look at all the hard books he has here.

Japanese: “ピエロ学・研究序説入門” “シャレたユーモア入門”
English: "Clowns for Dummies", "The Joke's On You",

Japanese: “部下のココロをつかむ入門” “ゆかいなジョーク入門”
English: "Treat Your Peons Right", And the classic "Funny Jokes Are Funny."

Japanese: うーん。ベンキョウ熱心だねえ。
English: Wow. Moe is very studious.

Japanese: “シャレたユーモア”‥‥ねえ。
English: "The Joke's On You"... Huh?

Japanese: なんか、ヘコんでるぞ、 てんじょうが。
English: Look at the ceiling... It looks like someone punched a hole in it!

Japanese: ホントだ。 ‥‥どうしたんだろうね。
English: You're right. I wonder what happened?

Japanese: （ナゾを解く手がかりは マドぎわにありそうだな）
English: (Hmm... I don't even want to imagine what goes on in here.)

Japanese: な、なあに このニンジン‥‥
English: What the!? There's a string of carrots here!

Japanese: いろんなカタチ、色の ニンジンがぶら下がっている。
English: How strange. The carrots seem to come in all different shapes... Weird...

Japanese: よっぽどニンジンがスキなのか、 何か使いみちでもあるのか‥‥？
English: I can't tell if Moe just likes carrots, or if he is using them for some sort of gag.

Japanese: ベッドの上に、ハデなパジャマが ハデにぬぎちらかされている。
English: Moe's got an excellent pair of pajamas... Laid on his bed in an excellent manner.

Japanese: うわ。パジャマなの？　コレ。
English: What? Those are pajamas?

Japanese: うーん。寝てるときも ピエロさんなんだねえ。
English: You mean he goes to bed dressed as a clown? Eww...

Japanese: ピエロの衣装がズラリ。 ある種、ブキミなながめだ。
English: All those clown costumes lined up like that... I dunno about you, but it's creepy.

Japanese: ひゃああ。スゴい コレクションだなあ。
English: Look at the collection he's got! It's incredible!

Japanese: 世界のピエロの衣装だね きっと。
English: It must be a collection of clown costumes from around the world.

Japanese: あッ、そうだ！
English: Oh! I almost forgot!

Japanese: な、なんだよ。 （またロクでもないコトを‥‥）
English: What is it now? (She better not want me to try one of those on!)

Japanese: あたしも集めようかなあ。
English: I was thinking of starting a costume collection myself!

Japanese: 世界の霊媒師の装束。 事務所にズラリとかざるの！
English: I'll call it "World Spirit Channels"... We can display it in our office!

Japanese: なんでぼくの事務所に かざるんだよ！
English: In OUR office!? As soon as you start paying the bills, you can say that.

Japanese: ‥‥あ。ここから現場が 見えるよ。
English: Nick, you can see the scene of the crime from here.

Japanese: （地面が荒らされたところ‥‥ ハッキリ見えるな）
English: (You can even tell that the ground has been disturbed...)

Japanese: すぐ目の前だよ。 １０メートルもないね。
English: It's right in front of this window, about 30 feet away.

Japanese: （事件を目撃しても フシギじゃない、か‥‥）
English: (I guess it wouldn't have been strange for someone to have seen the crime from here.)

Japanese: なんですか？ このバナナの皮‥‥
English: What's this? A banana peel?

Japanese: きゃああああッ！
English: *slips*

Japanese: おッ！　スジがいいね おぢょーさん！
English: Oh my! Oh my! What a wonderful fall girlie!

Japanese: バナナの皮を見たら、 とりあえずころんでおく！
English: You should remember that when you see a banana peel, get ready to fall down!

Japanese: これ、ピエロのキホンね。
English: That is one of the basic tenants of clownsmanship.

Japanese: いたたたた‥‥
English: Ow ow ow ow ow...

Japanese: オレっち、毎晩１００本、 かかさず練習してるんだぜ！
English: Every night, I fall down 100 times to practice the proper banana peel breakfall.

Japanese: ‥‥ほッ！
English: WHOA! *slip*

Japanese: ‥‥ほッ！‥‥はッ！

English: WHOA! *slip* WHOA! *slip*

Japanese: ‥‥ほッ！‥‥はッ！‥‥ふッ！
English: WHOA! *slip* WHOA! *slip* WHOA! *slip*

Japanese: はああ。やっぱりプロは ちがうねえ、なるほどくん。
English: Wow. He really is a pro, huh Nick!? His falls are totally different than my slip!

Japanese: いやー、やっぱりピエロは 芸ヒトスジだからさ。
English: Us clowns really take our work seriously.

Japanese: オレっちも日夜、いろんな コトにチャレンジしてるワケよ。
English: I try to hone my craft day and night, with the latest in jester technology.

Japanese: 一輪車、トランポリン、玉乗りに ツナわたり、アコーディオン。
English: Unicycles. Trampolines. Balancing Balls. Accordions.

Japanese: ことごとく ぶっコワれてますけど‥‥。
English: It looks like you've put quite a bit of wear and tear on that equipment...

Japanese: いやホレ、オレっちの 芸の永遠のテーマは、
English: Well... The theme of my act as a clown is...

Japanese: “いかにおもしろく コケるか”だからさ。
English: "How many ways can a fat funny guy fall down?"

Japanese: なんでもいいからコケときゃ いいワケよ。コケティッシュに。
English: I don't care what I break or how I break it, as long as it ends with me on my bum!!

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃひゃ。あひゃひゃ あひゃあひゃひゃあひゃ。ひゃ！
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: オレっち、ホラ。 ベンキョー家だからサ。
English: Take a look at my desk. Aren't I the most studious clown you've ever seen?

Japanese: ヒマなときは、 研究してるワケ。本を読んで。
English: Every free moment I have, I spend it studying or reading.

Japanese: “楽しいピエロ入門” “かならずウケる、かくし芸入門”
English: The classic "Pull My Finger" or "Clown Car Maintenance and Repair"...

Japanese: “都会的センスをみがく入門” “やさしい手品入門”
English: Not to mention the bestseller "Jean-Luc de Laduc's Guide To Obnoxious French Pierrots"...

Japanese: ‥‥なんだか、 “入門”ばっかりですね。
English: Umm... They all seem to cover rather basic topics though...

Japanese: それに、センモン外の本が まぎれこんでますよ。
English: Maybe he is just trying to cover all of his bases?

Japanese: ナニゴトもホレ。 ベンキョーだから。ベンキョー。
English: Exactly! I want to be a renaissance clown!

Japanese: （さすがに“楽しいピエロ入門”は ないんじゃないか‥‥？）
English: (Too bad he didn't buy "How To Be A Funny Clown"...)

Japanese: こわれてますね。 ‥‥てんじょう。
English: There's a hole in the ceiling... Why is there a hole in the ceiling?

Japanese: いやー。ちょっとさ。
English: Well... Umm...

Japanese: チョーシに乗って、 高く飛びすぎちゃって。
English: I was riding a pogo stick, and I guess I just overdid it a little...

Japanese: “飛びすぎた”‥‥？
English: "Overdid it a little"...?

Japanese: ま。ま。それはホレ。“ワカゲの 行ったり来たり”ってヤツ？
English: What can I say, sometimes things get a bit crazy in one's college days...
Huh?

Japanese: ニンジンはホレ、 オレっちの芸の目玉なワケよ。
English: Carrots are one of my trademark props.

Japanese: あ！　あたしたちも 見ましたよ！
English: Yep! I saw you use them!

Japanese: 目の前のニンジンを えんえん追っかけ回して、
English: You must believe in a carrot and no stick approach. You put the carrot out in front

Japanese: 最後に、ナマのまま まるかじりするヤツですよね。
English: for motivation, but I guess the trick is gauging what flavor you want each day...

Japanese: そう！　それそれ。 ツーカイだったろ？　な？
English: Exactly! You were really paying attention, weren't you!?

Japanese: 多い日は１日５本 食うワケだからさ。ニンジン。
English: You know, I eat about 5 of those carrots every single day.

Japanese: こうして、いろんな味の ニンジンを用意しておくワケ。
English: If you eat that many, you've gotta change up the taste every now and again.

Japanese: （味がちがうのか‥‥）
English: (I didn't understand any of that, nor do I ever want to.)

Japanese: ずいぶんハデな パジャマですよね。
English: Moe, you've got quite the collection of pajamas... They really scream out to you.

Japanese: そうかあ？ ジミだろう、それ。
English: You think they're loud, huh? I kinda thought they were refined...

Japanese: ジミすぎてステージで使えない から、パジャマにしてんだよ。
English: You know, a bit too adult for me to use on stage... That's why I used them as pajamas.

Japanese: （‥‥なんだ、ホントは 衣装なのか。ピエロの）
English: (Wait a second... He sleeps in clown costumes!?)

Japanese: オレっち、こう見えて オシャレだからさ。
English: I look awfully snazzy in these costumes.

Japanese: おしゃれ‥‥ですかあ。
English: Umm... Did you just use the word "snazzy" in a sentence?

Japanese: そう。世界中のピエロの衣装、 集めてるワケ。
English: I sure did. These are haute couture from the best international clown designers.

Japanese: ‥‥通信販売で。
English: At least, that's what they said on the TV shopping channel I use...

Japanese: その日の気分で決めるワケよ。 衣装と、ニンジンの大きさをサ。
English: I like having a big collection so I can match my costume to my mood... And my carrot.

Japanese: へええ。
English: Hehe...

Japanese: ま。私服はＴシャツ１枚しか 持ってないケド。
English: You know, I only own one T-shirt. The rest are all clown costumes!

Japanese: （それのどこが オシャレなんだ‥‥）
English: (Snazzy dresser!? More like spazzy dresser if you ask me!)

Japanese: サルのルーサーが 遊ぶための器具だろう。
English: Money must use this to swing around on...

Japanese: なるほどくんも あそんでいいよ。
English: Go ahead Nick. I know you want to ride on it too!

Japanese: ‥‥また今度にする。
English: Maybe later...

Japanese: バーベルに、ブルワーカー ‥‥とかいったっけ。
English: Hmm... He's got a barbell.

Japanese: おもに、上半身をきたえるための トレーニング機器だ。
English: Look around... Everything he's got is for upper body training.

Japanese: ‥‥テレビの通信販売で よく見かけるラインナップだな。
English: Wait a second... These are the same machines I see on TV all the time!

Japanese: なっ、なるほどくん！
English: Hey, Nick!

Japanese: どうした？
English: What?

Japanese: も、持てないよッ！ このバーベル！
English: I don't have this one! This barbell here is a new model!

Japanese: ‥‥ムリしないほうがいいよ。 ギックリ腰になるから。
English: Don't overdo it Maya. You don't want to end up a muscle woman.

Japanese: あーあ。コワれてるよ このカゴ。
English: Hey... The net's ripped.

Japanese: ルーサーくんだね、 こんなコトするの。
English: Money must be prone to breaking things... He's hardcore.

Japanese: ‥‥？　こわれてるって、 どこが？
English: Umm... The net looks fine to me. You really think that he plays basketball?

Japanese: え。だって、やぶけてるよ。 カゴの底が。
English: I think so... Monkeys live life above the rim you know.

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ 知らないの？　バスケット。
English: You're joking right? You think the monkey has got proverbial "game"?

Japanese: バスケット？　知ってるよ。 日本語で“カゴ”のコトでしょ？
English: Of course. That monkey doesn't fake the funk on a nasty dunk.

Japanese: （‥‥深く追求するのは また今度にしよう‥‥）
English: (Well, a prehensile tail might be an unfair advantage...)

Japanese: ひゃあ。ガラクタの山だよ。
English: Eww... It's a huge pile of trash!

Japanese: これがルーサーの お宝、ってわけか。
English: One man's trash is another monkey's treasure.

Japanese: たしかに、ピカピカ光るモノ ばっかりだね。
English: I guess you're right. Look! They're all shiny things.

Japanese: オートバイのマフラーまで あるぞ。
English: He's even got a motorcycle exhaust.

Japanese: あの楽器、なんていったっけ。 ‥‥ああ。チューバだチューバ。
English: What do they call this thing? Oh... A tuba, right?

Japanese: テューバだよ、なるほどくん。
English: Of course it's a tuba, silly! You didn't know that!?

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。 ミリカちゃんの写真だ。
English: Hey... It's a picture of Regina.

Japanese: うーん。彼女もある種、 キラキラしてるから。
English: Well, she is a shining beauty.

Japanese: そういうコトなのか‥‥？
English: No objections here.

Japanese: ベッドはキレイに ととのえられている。
English: This bed is incredibly well made. It's almost like a maid made it up.

Japanese: せんたくものまでが、 キチンとたたまれている。
English: Even the laundry on top of the bed is folded perfectly.

Japanese: これがあたりまえだよ なるほどくん。
English: Nick, there's nothing unusual about that at all. It's how things are supposed to be!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくにはできそうもないな。
English: Can't a man respect another man for doing something said man cannot do?

Japanese: あ。おサルさんの カレンダーだよ。
English: Hehe... It's a monkey calendar!

Japanese: よっぽどサル好きなんだろうね、 この部屋のヒト。
English: Whoever's in this room must really love monkeys.

Japanese: ‥‥それにしても。
English: Maybe a little too much...

Japanese: 今年ももう、終わりだねえ なるほどくん。
English: Wow... I just realized that the year is almost over. Amazing how time flies.

Japanese: いろいろあった１年だったな。 特に後半は。
English: It's been one wild year. Especially the last part...

Japanese: 今年中に助けてあげようね。 マックス。
English: Well, we still got one last person to help this year... Max.

Japanese: マドのそとから、 サーカスのテントが見える。
English: You can see the Big Top from the window here.

Japanese: テントの上、雪が つもってないね。
English: There is no snow on top of the Big Top... Kinda weird don't you think?

Japanese: テントの中、あったかかった からね。とけちゃったんだろ。
English: The inside of the tent is warm so any snow that lands on top probably melts.

Japanese: そうだねえ。テント、雪が つもったらつぶれちゃうもんね。
English: I guess you're right. The snow probably just slides right off.

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)
